assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: CODE CHANGE REQUEST' (line 9) and '## Action\nSub-task creation required.' (lines 21-23). subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory, confirming sub-task creation was triggered.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: SUGGESTION' (line 9). The Reasoning section (lines 13-18) explicitly calls out the suggestive language: '"should also add" and "would help". These phrases propose an enhancement rather than mandate a required change.' Key indicator #1 states: 'Suggestive language: "should also" (additive suggestion, not fixing something broken) and "would help" (benefit-oriented, not requirement-oriented)'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: NIT' (line 9) and 'No sub-task created.' (line 21). No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: QUESTION' (line 9) and 'No sub-task created.' (line 23). No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20). Only one sub-task was created (for comment 30001), and it contains all required template sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 31) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 32). The exact URL matches the required value.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 34) with '**Comment ID:** 30001', '**Author:** reviewer-a', '**File:** modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, line 60', and '**Comment:**' followed by the full original review comment text about transaction wrapping (lines 35-38).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only 6 .md files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, and report.md. No source code files (.rs, .toml, .json, etc.) exist in the outputs. The report does not mention any code file modifications. All outputs are classification files, sub-task descriptions, and the verification report.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have corresponding classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. The report's Review Feedback Summary table (report.md lines 27-31) lists all 4 comments with their classifications and actions. The report's Review Feedback check (line 5) summarizes: '1 code change request (comment 30001), 1 suggestion (comment 30002), 1 nit (comment 30003), 1 question (comment 30004)'.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md does not contain the word 'merge' anywhere. The overall verdict is 'FAIL' (line 17: '### Overall: FAIL'). No merge action or auto-merge directive appears in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` with 5 test functions covering deletion, 404, 409, include_deleted listing, and cascade verification. No modified or deleted test files. |'. Also confirmed in Style/Conventions section line 65: 'Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE): Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No modified or deleted test files.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a dedicated '### Convention Upgrade Check' section (lines 21-26) evaluating: 'CONVENTIONS.md match: No', 'Codebase pattern match: No', 'Performance-related scrutiny: Applied, but general database best practices are not sufficient for upgrade'. report.md Style/Conventions section (line 61) states: 'Convention Upgrade (PASS): Examined comment 30002 (index suggestion) for convention upgrade. No CONVENTIONS.md content available for the target repository. No demonstrable codebase pattern of consistent index creation in migration files.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md conclusion (line 27): 'This remains classified as SUGGESTION. No sub-task created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md Review Feedback Summary (line 29): '30002 | reviewer-a | ... | SUGGESTION | No sub-task (no project convention backs upgrade)'. The reasoning in review-30002.md explicitly notes 'No project convention backs an upgrade' (key indicator #3) and 'insufficient evidence of a consistent indexing pattern in migration files' (key indicator #4).

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row (line 13): 'Eval Quality: N/A'. report.md Style/Conventions section (line 64): 'Eval Quality (N/A): No eval result reviews found in the PR.' The Test Quality row shows PASS overall, indicating Eval Quality N/A did not negatively affect the combination. The report confirms no eval result reviews were detected.
